zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda de Bretagne於大約1060年出生在法國布列塔尼莫比韓斯艾福時，她的父親Bardolf Forfin Penthievre35歲，母親Orguen Hawise Normandy36歲。她和Walter Gautier de Eyncourt至少有4個兒子。她去世於1136年4月21日在英國林可爾史爾布藍可尼享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda de Bretagne was born about 1060, in Penthièvre, Morbihan, Brittany, France, her father, Bardolf Forfin Penthievre, was 35 and her mother, Orguen Hawise Normandy, was 36. She had at least 4 sons with Walter Gautier de Eyncourt. She died on 21 April 1136, in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda de Bretagne est née environ 1060, à Penthièvre, Morbihan, Bretagne, France, son père, Bardolf Forfin Penthievre, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Orguen Hawise Normandy, avait 36 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils avec Walter Gautier d’Eyncourt. Elle est décédée le 21 avril 1136, à Blankney, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda de Bretagne um 1060 in Penthièvre, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bardolf Forfin Penthievre 35 und ihre Mutter Orguen Hawise Normandy 36. Sie hatte mit Walter Gautier de Eyncourt mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 21. April 1136 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Blankney, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Bretagne è nata il circa 1060 nel Penthièvre, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, sua padre, Bardolf Forfin Penthievre, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Orguen Hawise Normandy, aveva 36 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli con Walter Gautier de Eyncourt. Morì il 21 aprile 1136, nel Blankney, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 77 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda de Bretagne(이)가 대략 1060년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 펜스이e브르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bardolf Forfin Penthievre(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Orguen Hawise Normandy(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 Walter Gautier de Eyncourt(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1136년 4월 21일 영국 링코실르 블랭니에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda de Bretagne nasceu aproximadamente 1060, em Penthièvre, Morbihan, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Bardolf Forfin Penthievre, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Orguen Hawise Normandy, tinha 36 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos com Walter Gautier de Eyncourt. Ela faleceu em 21 de abril de 1136, em Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda de Bretagne родилась около 1060 г. в местности пентиeвр, морбихань, бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Bardolf Forfin Penthievre было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Orguen Hawise Normandy, 36. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына от Walter Gautier de Eyncourt. Она умерла 21 апреля 1136 г. в местности бланни, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda de Bretagne nació aproximadamente 1060, en Penthièvre, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Bardolf Forfin Penthievre, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Orguen Hawise Normandy, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos con Walter Gautier de Eyncourt. Murió el 21 de abril de 1136, en Blankney, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años.
